files.note: NEW. Permanent flat 64-bit GDT + 64-bit TSS with 3 IST stacks (8 KiB each). init() fills IST tops, encodes the 16-byte TSS descriptor at the runtime address of TSS, lgdt, far-returns to reload CS=0x08, reloads DS/ES/SS/FS/GS=0x10, ltr 0x18. Descriptor bits quoted from OSDev GDT/TSS == Intel SDM Vol.3A 3.4.5/Fig.3-8, 7.2.3/Fig.7-4, 7.7/Fig.7-11 (verified via web.archive.org OSDev mirror; live OSDev is Cloudflare-gated). TSS is a [u32;26] (not a packed u64 struct) so every field stays naturally aligned (no unaligned_references hazard). Exposes pub(super) KERNEL_CODE_SEL/KERNEL_DATA_SEL/TSS_SEL, DescriptorPointer, init().

NEW. 256 16-byte 64-bit interrupt-gate descriptors (selector 0x08, type_attr 0x8E = P=1/DPL=0/64-bit interrupt gate). init() fills every gate from trap::thunk(v), routes #DF(8)->IST1, NMI(2)->IST2, #MC(18)->IST3, then lidt. Gate layout quoted from OSDev IDT §x86-64 == Intel SDM Vol.3A 6.14.1/Fig.6-8; IST per 6.14.5. Exposes pub(super) init(); reuses gdt::DescriptorPointer.

NEW. global_asm! emits 256 per-vector thunks (.altmacro/.rept; dummy 0 errcode for non-error vectors, none for 8/10/11/12/13/14/17/21/30 per SDM Table 6-1), __alltraps (pushes 15 GPRs -> TrapFrame, mov rdi,rsp [RSP is 16-aligned at the call], call x86_trap_handler, restore, add rsp,16, iretq), and the __trap_thunks address table. x86_trap_handler is the ONLY raw-frame deref: builds crate::TrapInfo (cause = vector<<32|errcode32, fault_addr=CR2 on #PF, pc=rip), calls crate::dispatch_trap; Resume -> returns (iretq; #BP RIP already past int3 per SDM 6.15), Halt -> super::halt(). Also pub fn breakpoint() = int3. EXTERNAL CONTRACT (must exist at crate root in lib.rs): pub struct TrapInfo{pub kind:TrapKind,pub cause:u64,pub fault_addr:u64,pub pc:u64}; pub enum TrapKind{Breakpoint,PageFault,Undefined,Other}; pub enum TrapAction{Resume,Halt}; pub fn set_trap_hook(f: fn(&TrapInfo)->TrapAction); pub(crate) fn dispatch_trap(info:&TrapInfo)->TrapAction (reads the registered hook, default Halt).

UPDATED (full file). Adds `pub mod gdt; pub mod idt; pub mod trap;`, re-exports `breakpoint`, and adds idempotent `install_traps()` (AtomicBool guard; calls gdt::init() THEN idt::init() — order matters because the IDT's IST indices select TSS stacks the GDT installs). Keeps the M0 surface verbatim: `pub mod boot; pub mod serial;`, `pub use serial::{serial_init, serial_write_byte};`, and `pub fn halt() -> !`. SIBLING WIRING REQUIRED (not my files): (1) crates/tb-hal/src/arch/mod.rs add to the x86_64 AND aarch64 cfg re-export lines: `install_traps, breakpoint`. (2) crates/tb-hal/src/lib.rs add the public API + types + hook: TrapInfo/TrapKind/TrapAction, `set_trap_hook`, `pub(crate) fn dispatch_trap` (default Halt), `pub fn install_traps(){arch::install_traps()}`, `pub fn breakpoint(){arch::breakpoint()}`.

NEW. global_asm! VBAR_EL1 table: 2KiB-aligned, 16x128B slots; slot 4 (offset 0x200, Current-EL-SPx Sync) -> real handler __vec_sync, all 15 others -> __vec_other stub. SAVE_CONTEXT/RESTORE_CONTEXT macros marshal x0..x30 + ELR_EL1 + SPSR_EL1 into a 0x110 frame; trampolines do mov x0,sp / set x1=source / bl aarch64_trap_handler / restore / eret. Section .text._vectors (KEEP'd by linker). Verified: assembles under clang LLVM-IAS and links 2KiB-aligned at 0x40080800 with the real aarch64.ld.

NEW. The only place that derefs the raw exception frame. extern "C" aarch64_trap_handler(frame, source) reads ESR_EL1 (EC=bits[31:26]) + FAR_EL1, builds a safe crate::TrapInfo, classifies BRK64=0x3C->Breakpoint / UNKNOWN=0x00->Undefined / DABT 0x24|0x25->PageFault / else Other, calls crate::dispatch_trap(&info); on Resume of a brk advances frame.elr by 4, on Halt calls crate::halt(). breakpoint() does brk #0. TrapFrame layout locked by const offset_of!/size_of asserts (size 0x110, elr 0xF8, spsr 0x100). NOTE: depends on lib.rs (other agent) providing pub TrapInfo/TrapKind/TrapAction, pub fn set_trap_hook, and pub(crate) fn dispatch_trap (contract documented in the module note). EC values cited from Linux arch/arm64/include/asm/esr.h.

EDITED (full file). Adds `mod trap;` and `mod vectors;`, re-exports `breakpoint` from trap, and adds `pub fn install_traps()` which arms VBAR_EL1 (adrp/add :lo12: __exception_vectors; msr vbar_el1; isb) -- idempotent (the same table _start pre-armed). M0 `halt()` preserved verbatim. install_traps + breakpoint must also be re-exported by arch/mod.rs (other agent) for the aarch64 cfg arm, and wrapped by lib.rs.

EDITED (full file). Removed the old M0 spin-on-fault `__exception_vectors` global_asm! block (the real table now lives in vectors.rs with the same symbol -- verified single definition, no duplicate-symbol link error). `_start` is otherwise unchanged and still pre-arms VBAR_EL1 with `__exception_vectors` (now the real M1 table) before branching to rust_main, so M0 boot is preserved and early faults are vectored. Linked clean against the production kernel/linker/aarch64.ld with __exception_vectors landing 2KiB-aligned at 0x40080800.

OWNED (arch-neutral facade). Adds the M1 trap surface: install_traps(), breakpoint(), set_trap_hook(), pub(crate) dispatch_trap(), and the safe TrapInfo/TrapKind/TrapAction ABI. Static hook stored as AtomicUsize (0 = default halt hook); dispatch_trap transmutes usize->fn pointer (the one unsafe). Matches the EXACT contract the sibling-owned x86_64/trap.rs and aarch64/trap.rs call into (verified: both use crate::dispatch_trap, crate::TrapInfo{kind,cause,fault_addr,pc}, crate::TrapKind/TrapAction, halt()). All M0 symbols preserved.

OWNED (arch-neutral dispatch). Extended to re-export the new M1 symbols install_traps + breakpoint (alongside halt/serial_*) from whichever cfg-selected arch backend. REQUIRED for lib.rs to compile: the sibling-owned arch/x86_64/mod.rs and arch/aarch64/mod.rs both state they are 're-exported by arch/mod.rs' and define pub fn install_traps + pub use trap::breakpoint. Verified those symbols exist in both sibling mod.rs files.

OWNED. Full M1 test sequence: keeps the M0 'hello from rust_main' line, then install_traps(); set_trap_hook(trap_hook); 'trap-test: triggering breakpoint'; breakpoint(); 'trap-test: resumed past breakpoint'; 'M1: traps OK' (DoD marker); halt(). The safe hook fn(&TrapInfo)->TrapAction matches Breakpoint=>print+Resume, else=>print cause/fault_addr/pc + Halt (with a safe no_std hex printer). Panic handler kept. No unsafe blocks (only the #[unsafe(no_mangle)] attribute exception, as in M0).
OWNED. Only change vs M0: MARKER='M1: traps OK' (the new DoD) plus the matching header-comment line. All QEMU microvm/PVH launch logic, KVM/TCG selection, timeout and grep verdict unchanged.
files.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# scripts/run-x86_64.sh - QEMU launcher + Definition-of-Done check for M0 (x86_64).
#
# Boots the PVH ELF kernel under QEMU 'microvm' (the machine type Firecracker is
# modelled on), wires legacy 16550 COM1 to stdio, and asserts the EXACT marker
# "M1: traps OK" appears on serial. Fail-closed: a wall-clock timeout
# always bounds the run (the kernel halts rather than exiting), and a missing
# marker is a non-zero exit.
#
# PVH is selected automatically by QEMU from the XEN_ELFNOTE_PHYS32_ENTRY note
# in the ELF (see crates/tb-hal/src/arch/x86_64/boot.rs + kernel/linker/x86_64.ld).
#   refs: https://xenbits.xen.org/docs/unstable/misc/pvh.html
#         https://www.qemu.org/docs/master/system/i386/microvm.html
#
# Usage:   scripts/run-x86_64.sh [path/to/kernel-elf]
# Env:     QEMU=...  QEMU_TIMEOUT=<secs>  PROFILE=debug|release
set -euo pipefail

REPO_ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/.." && pwd)"
TARGET="x86_64-tabos-none"
PROFILE="${PROFILE:-debug}"
KERNEL="${1:-${REPO_ROOT}/target/${TARGET}/${PROFILE}/tabos-kernel}"
MARKER='M1: traps OK'
TIMEOUT_SECS="${QEMU_TIMEOUT:-15}"
QEMU="${QEMU:-qemu-system-x86_64}"

if ! command -v "${QEMU}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  echo "error: ${QEMU} not found on PATH (install qemu-system-x86 in WSL2)" >&2
  exit 2
fi
if [[ ! -f "${KERNEL}" ]]; then
  echo "error: kernel image not found: ${KERNEL}" >&2
  echo "build it first, e.g.:" >&2
  echo "  cargo build -p tabos-kernel --target ${REPO_ROOT}/targets/${TARGET}.json" >&2
  exit 2
fi

# Prefer KVM only when /dev/kvm is actually usable; otherwise pure-TCG so this
# runs in any WSL2 / CI box without nested virt.
ACCEL="tcg"
CPU="qemu64"
if [[ -e /dev/kvm && -r /dev/kvm && -w /dev/kvm ]]; then
  ACCEL="kvm"
  CPU="host"
fi

echo ">> qemu=${QEMU} accel=${ACCEL} cpu=${CPU} timeout=${TIMEOUT_SECS}s" >&2
echo ">> kernel=${KERNEL}" >&2

set +e
OUTPUT="$(timeout --foreground "${TIMEOUT_SECS}" \
  "${QEMU}" \
    -M microvm,rtc=off \
    -accel "${ACCEL}" -cpu "${CPU}" -m 256M -smp 1 \
    -kernel "${KERNEL}" \
    -no-reboot \
    -serial stdio -display none 2>&1)"
RC=$?
set -e

printf '%s\n' "${OUTPUT}"

if printf '%s' "${OUTPUT}" | grep -qF -- "${MARKER}"; then
  echo ">> PASS: observed marker '${MARKER}'" >&2
  exit 0
fi

echo ">> FAIL: marker '${MARKER}' not seen (qemu/timeout rc=${RC})" >&2
exit 1

files.note: OWNED. Only change vs M0: MARKER="M1: traps OK" (the new DoD) plus the matching header-comment line. All QEMU virt launch logic, timeout and grep verdict unchanged.
files.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# scripts/run-aarch64.sh -- QEMU `virt` first-light runner (milestone M0).
#
# Boots the aarch64 tb-os image under QEMU, captures the PL011 serial stream,
# and asserts the executable Definition-of-Done marker "M1: traps OK".
# Doubles as the cargo runner for target aarch64-tabos-none (cargo passes the
# freshly built ELF as $1) and is runnable by hand on WSL2.
#
# Invocation sources:
#   * QEMU `virt` board: UART0 = ARM PL011 @ 0x0900_0000, FDT pointer in x0,
#     `-kernel <ELF>` enters at EL1 (QEMU docs; dtb dumped via -machine dumpdtb).
#   * `-nographic` already routes the serial onto stdio AND muxes the monitor;
#     additionally passing `-serial stdio` makes QEMU abort with
#     "cannot use stdio by multiple character devices", so we deliberately do
#     not pass both -- `-nographic` *is* the requested serial-on-stdio path.
set -euo pipefail

REPO_ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/.." && pwd)"
PROFILE="${PROFILE:-debug}"
KERNEL="${1:-${REPO_ROOT}/target/aarch64-tabos-none/${PROFILE}/tabos-kernel}"
QEMU="${QEMU_AARCH64:-qemu-system-aarch64}"
MARKER="M1: traps OK"
TIMEOUT_SECS="${QEMU_TIMEOUT:-15}"

if ! command -v "${QEMU}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "[run-aarch64] error: '${QEMU}' not found on PATH" >&2
    exit 127
fi

# -M virt          : QEMU AArch64 'virt' machine (PL011 @ 0x09000000, GICv2, FDT)
# -cpu cortex-a72  : real A72; guest entry = EL1h, MMU off, DAIF masked, x0=FDT
# -m 128M          : virt RAM = 0x4000_0000..0x4800_0000; image links at +512 KiB
# -nographic       : headless; PL011 -> this terminal's stdio (see note above)
# -no-reboot       : do not loop on a fatal guest event
# -kernel <ELF>    : load PT_LOADs at p_paddr, jump to e_entry (= _start)
set +e
OUTPUT="$(timeout --foreground "${TIMEOUT_SECS}" \
    "${QEMU}" \
        -M virt \
        -cpu cortex-a72 \
        -m 128M \
        -nographic \
        -no-reboot \
        -kernel "${KERNEL}" \
    < /dev/null 2>&1)"
QEMU_RC=$?
set -e

printf '%s\n' "${OUTPUT}"

if printf '%s' "${OUTPUT}" | grep -qF -- "${MARKER}"; then
    echo "[run-aarch64] PASS -- observed DoD marker: '${MARKER}'"
    exit 0
fi

echo "[run-aarch64] FAIL -- marker '${MARKER}' not seen" >&2
echo "[run-aarch64]   (qemu exit=${QEMU_RC}; the kernel halts in wfi, so a" >&2
echo "[run-aarch64]    ${TIMEOUT_SECS}s timeout/exit=124 is expected -- the grep is the verdict)" >&2
exit 1

issues.problem: breakpoint() executes `asm!("brk #0", options(nomem, nostack, preserves_flags))`. The `nomem` option tells the compiler no memory is read/written across the instruction, but `brk #0` transfers control to aarch64_trap_handler, whose policy hook is free to write memory (e.g. serial MMIO, and in general any caller-visible state a debugger-style handler touches). This makes a public trap primitive technically unsound and diverges from the x86_64 sibling, which correctly uses a conservative bare `asm!("int3")` (implicit memory clobber). It does NOT break the M1 DoD because the M1 hook only writes serial/its own frame, none of which rust_main caches across the call, but it is a latent footgun.
issues.fix: Drop the `nomem` option so the compiler treats the trap as a memory barrier, matching the conservative x86_64 `int3`: change to `core::arch::asm!("brk #0", options(nostack, preserves_flags));` (or simply `core::arch::asm!("brk #0");`). `preserves_flags` remains accurate since the handler restores SPSR_EL1/NZCV.

reviews.verdict: No blockers found — the M1 breakpoint trap path is correct on both architectures for the DoD. x86_64: the dummy-errcode vector set (8,10,11,12,13,14,17,21,30) is complete and correctly excludes vector 3, so #BP pushes a dummy 0; the 15-GPR push order matches struct TrapFrame and the reverse pops + `add rsp,16` + `iretq` are sound; long-mode force-aligns RSP to 16 before the iret push and both errcode/non-errcode cases leave RSP 16-aligned at the `call`; IDT gate type_attr=0x8E and selector=0x08 are correct, IST routing 8/2/18 -> IST1/2/3 matches the TSS (IST offsets 0x24/0x2C/0x34, IOPB=sizeof disabled, access 0x89 long-mode descriptor), TR is loaded via ltr, and ist_top returns 16-aligned exclusive tops; #BP's saved RIP already points past int3 so Resume needs no fixup. aarch64: brk from EL1h hits Sync-SPx slot 0x200; SAVE/RESTORE offsets match the const-asserted TrapFrame (elr@0xF8, spsr@0x100), SP stays 16-aligned, x9/x10 are reused only after being saved and elr/spsr are restored before GPR reload; ESR_EL1.EC==0x3C classifies the brk and the handler advances frame.elr by 4 (visible because frame==sp), so eret resumes past the brk with no infinite loop. Linker scripts keep .text.trap/.rodata (x86) and KEEP .text._vectors (aarch64), and VBAR/IDTR/GDTR/TR are all installed in the right order. The single reported item is a minor latent-soundness nit on the aarch64 breakpoint() asm options that does not affect M1.

PASS - no build-correctness blockers found. The M1 trap code compiles and links cleanly on top of the working M0 setup. I read all files in full (including the parts truncated in the prompt: x86_64 trap.rs handler/TrapFrame/thunk/breakpoint, gdt.rs init/load_gdt_and_segments, lib.rs hook storage, aarch64 RESTORE_CONTEXT/handler) plus both Cargo.toml editions, both target JSONs, both linker scripts, and .cargo/config.toml. Every item on the requested checklist holds. A live build here was inconclusive only because this machine has stable Rust 1.92.0 with no rustup/rust-src, so -Zbuild-std cannot activate (error E0463 can't find core) - an environment limitation, not a code error.

Verified clean:
1) Editions are 2021 for BOTH tb-hal and tabos-kernel (not 2024), so trap.rs's plain `#[no_mangle] pub(super) extern "C" fn x86_trap_handler` and the plain `extern "C" { static __trap_thunks: [usize;256]; }` block compile fine (they would only be hard errors under edition 2024, which is not in use). The kernel's `#[unsafe(no_mangle)]` is also valid in edition 2021 on the pinned nightly.
2) AtomicUsize hook storage (lib.rs TRAP_HOOK) and AtomicBool (x86_64/mod.rs TRAPS_INSTALLED): max-atomic-width is 64 (x86_64) and 128 (aarch64), both >= the 64-bit usize, so both atomics lower fine. The usize<->fn-pointer transmute lives in tb-hal (unsafe allowed there), not the kernel.
3) TrapFrame field order EXACTLY matches the __alltraps push order: struct is rax,rbx,rcx,rdx,rsi,rdi,rbp,r8..r15,vector,error_code,rip,cs,rflags,rsp,ss; pushes put rax lowest and the thunk pushes errcode-then-vector so vector sits at the lower address (matching field order). pop sequence mirrors the push, and `add rsp,16` discards exactly vector+errcode for both error-code and dummy-errcode paths. Stack is 16-aligned at the `call` in both paths.
4) extern "C" signatures match the asm: x86_trap_handler(*mut TrapFrame) gets rdi=rsp; aarch64_trap_handler(*mut TrapFrame, u64) gets x0=sp, x1=source.
5) global_asm! syntax is valid: GAS .altmacro/%vecidx, .if with `==`/`||`, `.section .text.trap/.rodata "...",@progbits`, `.quad __trap_thunk_N` table; no naked-fn pitfalls (entry stubs are pure global_asm, handlers are ordinary extern "C"). gdt.rs's inline-asm CS reload (push CS, lea [rip+2f], push, retfq, segment reloads via {data:x}, ltr {tss:x}) is stack-balanced and register-correct.
6) All submodules are declared and wired: x86_64/mod.rs declares gdt/idt/trap and calls gdt::init() then idt::init() in the required order; init fns are pub(super) and reachable; breakpoint is pub and re-exported.
7) Linking: x86_64.ld `*(.text .text.*)` catches `.text.trap` and `*(.rodata .rodata.*)` catches the `__trap_thunks` table; aarch64.ld KEEPs `.text._vectors` and catches `.text/.rodata`. relocation-model=static + ET_EXEC resolves the `.quad` thunk addresses at link time.
8) Kernel stays unsafe-free in body (only the `#[unsafe(no_mangle)]` attribute, by documented design); int3 is in tb-hal trap.rs and brk #0 in aarch64 trap.rs; the safe trap_hook returns TrapAction and never touches unsafe.

issues.problem: breakpoint() executes `asm!("brk #0", options(nomem, nostack, preserves_flags))`. The `nomem` option tells the compiler no memory is read/written across the instruction, but `brk #0` transfers control to aarch64_trap_handler, whose policy hook is free to write memory (e.g. serial MMIO, and in general any caller-visible state a debugger-style handler touches). This makes a public trap primitive technically unsound and diverges from the x86_64 sibling, which correctly uses a conservative bare `asm!("int3")` (implicit memory clobber). It does NOT break the M1 DoD because the M1 hook only writes serial/its own frame, none of which rust_main caches across the call, but it is a latent footgun.
issues.fix: Drop the `nomem` option so the compiler treats the trap as a memory barrier, matching the conservative x86_64 `int3`: change to `core::arch::asm!("brk #0", options(nostack, preserves_flags));` (or simply `core::arch::asm!("brk #0");`). `preserves_flags` remains accurate since the handler restores SPSR_EL1/NZCV.
